rationale: Abstract X precisely articulates the ICE-specific knowledge gap, reports results with full denominators and follow-up distribution, and calibrates its conclusion to the feasibility design with explicit calls for confirmatory study, whereas Abstract Y uses vague gap framing and an under-caveated conclusion.
loser_weakness: Abstract Y lacked certainty calibration — the conclusion omitted acknowledgment of the hypothesis-generating nature of the small series and provided no caveat that larger comparative studies are needed before routine adoption.
